commit.message:
Slim CLAUDE.md and make command files self-contained

Move all command-specific instructions from CLAUDE.md (~275 lines) into
their respective .claude/commands/ files. CLAUDE.md now only contains
project overview, structure, and shared conventions (~28 lines).

Each command file specifies which project files to read, reducing
unnecessary context loading (e.g. /review no longer reads grammar.md
and vocabulary.md, /redo reads no project files).

Also updates:
- review.md: inline grading format, error tracker + level.md steps
- listen.md: Python-only helper script, anti-spoiling rules
- drill.md: German→Italian only format

commit.message:
eval: add 14-game held-out blind test — avg 90% structural

Add golden samples for 14 unseen games (2048, popstar, watermelon, jump,
breakout, minesweeper, piano_tiles, fruit_ninja, whack_mole, match3,
space_shooter, sokoban, pinball, circle_pin) to validate generalization.

Results: held-out avg 90% vs training avg 94% (4% gap, no overfitting).
Top tier (>=95%): 5 games. System weakness: over-design on complex games.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: tags on first child node — remove L1 root tagging

write_memory now stores tags on the first child node (not the root).
L1 is always visible in bulk reads, so root-level tags add no discovery
value. Sub-node tags power findRelated aggregation.

If write_memory has no child nodes, tags are silently discarded.

commit.message:
Redesign: Volume-only OSD with glassy modern UI and adaptive polling

Major Changes:
- Minimalist volume-only OSD (removed full mode with source/sound info)
- Modern glassy design with subtle colors and transparency
- Compact layout (240x40dp) centered at bottom
- Removed icons/emojis, text-only labels (VOL/MUTE)
- Volume display: Always 0-98 scale (Denon relative mode)

Performance:
- Adaptive polling: 150ms during volume changes, 400ms otherwise
- Faster animation: 120ms (smooth with 150ms polling)
- Only fetch lite XML (power/volume/mute)
- Turbo mode: Auto-activates for 3s after volume/mute changes

Code Cleanup:
- AVRState: Minimal fields (power, volumeDb, muted)
- OSDView: Single drawVolume() method, removed drawFull()
- AVRClient: Simplified polling, removed full XML fetch
- OSDTrigger: Only VOLUME and MUTE triggers
- ~200 lines of code removed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix(report): enforce ## headings in LLM prompt to prevent bold-only section titles

The report generator prompt was not specifying markdown heading levels for
section titles. Gemini would sometimes use **bold text** or ### instead of ##,
causing parseSections() to find zero sections and falling back to wall-of-text.

Changes:
- Prefix all section titles in REPORT STRUCTURE with ## (H2) and ### (H3)
- Add CRITICAL FORMATTING RULE block at top of REPORT STRUCTURE
- Add reminder in ADDITIONAL GUIDELINES
- Fix .gitignore: add !web-platform/lib/ exception so web-platform/lib/ is tracked

kontekstin ulkopuolelle)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(async): prevent crashes from async callbacks after Fragment destruction

Critical safety fixes for async callbacks that could access destroyed Views:

1. CameraFragment:
   - Added _binding null check in ImageCapture.OnImageSavedCallback
   - Added _binding null check in startCamera() listener
   - Prevents crashes when camera callbacks fire after Fragment destruction

2. VoiceFragment:
   - Added _binding null checks in ALL RecognitionListener callbacks
   - Safe navigation for binding access in onError/onResults/onPartialResults
   - Prevents crashes when speech recognition completes after Fragment exit

These callbacks run asynchronously and could fire after the user navigates
away from the Fragment, causing NullPointerException crashes. Now all
callbacks safely check _binding before accessing Views.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Close Phase 1 — "To All the Girls"

Ehrenfest v0.1 spec is complete. Three .paul binary programs compile
end-to-end through Afana to valid OpenQASM 2.0/3.0.

Serde field mapping rewritten to match CDDL schema:
- PauliOp serializes as integers (I=0, X=1, Y=2, Z=3)
- PauliTerm uses coefficient/paulis (was coeff/ops)
- Hamiltonian gains constant_offset
- EvolutionTime uses total_us/steps/dt_us (was time/trotter_steps/trotter_order)
- NoiseConstraint: t1_us/t2_us required, adds readout_fidelity_min
- Observable is a tagged enum (SZ/SX/E/rho/F)
- SystemDef gains optional backend_hint and cooling_profile
- TrotterOrder moved from schema to compiler flag

New files:
- examples/rabi.paul, ising.paul, heisenberg.paul — binary CBOR
- afana/tests/parse_examples.rs — parse + validate physics values
- afana/tests/roundtrip.rs — CBOR serialize/deserialize equality
- afana/tests/compile_examples.rs — full pipeline → QASM3
- spec/FORMAT.md — CBOR encoding rules, field key table
- spec/LANGUAGE.md — prose semantics (physics, not gates)

File extension: .paul (Paul's Boutique Phase 1 tracklist).
28 afana tests pass (18 unit + 10 integration).

Co-Authored-By: Daniel Hinderink <daniel@hinderink.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: pass --force to officellm init to overwrite stale skill files (#328)

The officellm CLI skips existing files by default during init. This
caused SKILL.md to stay at the old version even after the binary
upgraded. Pass --force so init always overwrites existing assets.

Closes #327

feat: SSE + Redis — real-time job streaming, persistent job storage

- jobStore.js: all job operations backed by Redis with 24h TTL
- New SSE endpoint GET /job/:jobId/stream — server pushes events
  the instant they happen (log, stage, done, failed)
- Heartbeat every 20s to survive proxy timeouts
- Subscribe before catch-up read to prevent race conditions

Frontend:
- Replace 800ms setInterval polling with EventSource
- Incrementally builds job state from pushed events

perf: eliminate String allocations in topology cache synapse lookups (#769) (#779)

* perf: eliminate String allocations in topology cache synapse lookups (#769)

Replace flat HashSet<(String, String)> and HashMap<(String, String), f32>
with nested HashMap<String, HashSet<String>> and HashMap<String, HashMap<String, f32>>
to enable zero-copy &str lookups in synapse_exists() and synapse_weight().

Benchmarks show 25-42% improvement in pure lookup cost at 500 neurons.

fix(gateway-api): ignore entire v1alpha2 TLSRoute object in ignoreDifferences

Cilium 1.19.x requires TLSRoute v1alpha2 but Gateway API v1.4.1 standard
channel marks it as served=false. Previous jqPathExpression selected only
the .served leaf value which doesn't work with Replace=true +
RespectIgnoreDifferences=true. Selecting the entire v1alpha2 object ensures
ArgoCD copies the live state (served=true) on Replace.

commit.message:
safety: block rm -rf targeting critical system directories

Extend isDangerousRm to flag rm -rf targeting /etc, /usr, /var, /boot,
/bin, /sbin, /lib, /proc, /sys. These paths have no legitimate role in
Bloom's development context.

Uses boundary-aware regex to avoid false positives on paths like
/home/user/etc-notes or /usr/local/share/myapp (deep subpaths).

Adds 7 new tests including 2 negative tests (153 -> 160).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: allow bare git-reset with hard flag (equivalent to HEAD)

The previous regex incorrectly blocked the bare form (no argument)
because the negative lookahead required \s+HEAD to follow the flag.
When nothing follows, the lookahead fails and the command was blocked.

Fix: require a space + argument after the flag before checking the
lookahead, so the bare form doesn't match at all (allowed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Reviewfix: auto-request Copilot re-review after pushing fix commit (Forge-bj3) (#34)

* feat: auto-request Copilot re-review after reviewfix pushes fix commit

After reviewfix.Fix() successfully pushes a fix commit, call
gh pr review-request to notify the reviewer (GitHub Copilot by default)
so the review cycle continues. Without this, bellows never sees another
EventReviewChanges trigger and the loop stalls.

Changes:
- ghpr: add RequestReReview() wrapping gh pr review-request
- reviewfix: add Reviewer field to FixParams (defaults to copilot-pull-request-reviewer)
- reviewfix: call RequestReReview after successful fix + log EventReReviewRequested
- state: add EventReReviewRequested event type
- docs/reviewfix.md: document the reviewfix loop and re-review mechanism

Bead: Forge-bj3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add unit tests for reviewfix package

Tests cover filterActionableComments (all state transitions), buildReviewFixPrompt
(comment rendering with/without author and path), and DefaultCopilotReviewer constant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use correct event type when re-review request fails after fix

Using EventReviewFixFailed when the fix succeeded but the re-review
request failed was semantically incorrect. Add EventReReviewRequestFailed
event type and use it in reviewfix to accurately represent the failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-bj3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
